steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence makes a main claim (processor power has drastically increased) and then elaborates with a surprising consequence introduced by "even" — even once-revolutionary dual-core technology is now commonplace. The punctuation must correctly attach that elaboration to the main clause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘thousands, that’: Creates a comma splice with no coordinating conjunction. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘thousands that-’: The punctuation pattern is grammatically invalid. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘thousands - that’: A dash correctly introduces the elaborating clause; "that" marks it as subordinate, making the whole construction grammatical. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘thousands. That’: Splits the elaboration into a fragment — "That the once-revolutionary..." has no predicate. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
